steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence introduces a three-genre classification system. The second sentence names specific plays and their genres. The blank must signal that the second sentence is giving a concrete example of that system — not adding to it, contrasting it, or comparing it to something else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘in addition,’: Signals that a new, separate point is being added. But the second sentence is not adding a new idea — it is illustrating the classification system just described. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘similarly,’: Signals a comparison between two things that resemble each other. There is only one classification system; nothing is being compared to it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘for instance,’: Signals that what follows is an example of what was just stated. Much Ado About Nothing classified as comedy and Richard II as history are examples of the three-genre system in action. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘however,’: Signals a contrast or contradiction. The second sentence does not contradict the first — it supports it with examples. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
